byte_budget_probe.verdict: smooth-basis budget within target band; random-basis pessimistic but still affordable. Real Fisher eigenvectors (smoothed by SegNet stride-2 stem + PoseNet preprocessing) expected to land in smooth-basis regime.

fisher_rank_synthetic_check.spectrum_decay: from 0.218 at top to 1e-8 at rank-48
fisher_rank_synthetic_check.verdict: VERIFIED — Council Section 5.3 low-rank claim holds on synthetic fixture. Real upstream (EfficientNet-B2 SegNet + FastViT-T12 PoseNet) verification deferred to CUDA: each Fisher matvec needs JVP+VJP through both models which is too heavy on CPU/MPS.

step_2_compress_time_basis_build.blocker: needs renderer output + GT pair tensors (Lane G v3 owv3_0120 anchor — extract via existing inflate_renderer pipeline)

claude_md_compliance.no_mps_strategic_decisions: OK — all SJ-KL evidence currently tagged [prediction] or [synthetic-fixture]; no contest-CUDA score claimed
claude_md_compliance.no_cli_invented_flags: OK — build_sjkl_residual.py argparse self-consistent (no subprocess invocations into other tools)
claude_md_compliance.additive_not_replacement: OK — apply_sjkl_at_decode is purely additive on fake1/fake2 (mirrors pr67's actuator add pattern); does NOT replace existing components
claude_md_compliance.strict_scorer_rule: OK — basis is shipped IN archive; no scorer load at decode time

simpler_parameterization_findings.subagent_finding: Fisher rank ≤ scorer-output-dim bottleneck (~few thousand for real upstream) means eigenvectors are ALWAYS LOW-FREQUENCY (smoothed through SegNet stride-2 stem and PoseNet downsample). Storing them at 32x24 coarse grid + bilinear-upsample saves 196x storage with negligible reconstruction loss. ALSO: scale is sqrt(λ) eigenvalue prior — keeping the prior decoupled from the unit-normalized basis lets us re-encode alpha at different bit depths without recomputing the basis.
simpler_parameterization_findings.decision: Council Section 5.3's coarse-grid suggestion was already in the spec; subagent confirmed it is empirically necessary to hit the ~13KB byte budget
